With rookie Kurtis Rourke gaining momentum internally, speculation is growing that Jones could be moved before the trade deadline. The Indianapolis Colts, Arizona Cardinals and Atlanta Falcons have emerged as potential landing spots for the veteran backup.

articles.title: Goals galore! Super ball, extra time and expanded field trigger record-breaking goal rush at World Cup 2026
articles.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is shaping up to be an exhilarating showdown, with a remarkable 25% increase in goal scores compared to the last tournament. Innovations in ball technology and prolonged match durations are driving this offensive surge. Key players from top leagues, including stars from Real Madrid and Liverpool, along with Lionel Messi's Inter Miami, are leading the scoreboard, anticipating record-breaking goal counts.

articles.title: Cristiano Ronaldo's fiancée Georgina Rodríguez leaves court victorious after explosive family dispute
articles.description: Cristiano Ronaldo's fiancée Georgina Rodríguez has won a major legal battle in Spain after a court ruled that comments made during a 2023 television interview violated her right to honor and privacy. The court ordered broadcaster Atresmedia to pay Georgina €80,000 and her sister Ivana €40,000 in compensation. The ruling has reignited debate over celebrity privacy, media responsibility, and the legal limits of discussing public figures' personal lives.

articles.title: "Turning into a kpop event": FIFA's bold BTS halftime show move sparks controversy ahead of World Cup final
articles.description: FIFA's announcement of Madonna, Shakira and BTS as performers for the 2026 World Cup final halftime show has sparked intense online debate. While many fans welcomed the star-studded lineup, others argued football is becoming too focused on entertainment. BTS supporters defended the group's inclusion, citing Jungkook's 2022 World Cup performance and the band's global appeal, while critics questioned FIFA's growing emphasis on K-pop and American-style spectacle.

articles.title: NFL Trade Rumors: Dallas Cowboys' $6 Million WR linked to Kansas City Chiefs in blockbuster trade prediction
articles.description: George Pickens has been linked with the Kansas City Chiefs in a blockbuster trade prediction ahead of the 2026 NFL trade deadline. While the Dallas Cowboys wide receiver recently dismissed holdout concerns, one analyst believes Kansas City could pursue him if Dallas falls out of playoff contention. The Chiefs' need for another proven receiver and Pickens' uncertain long-term future with the Cowboys continue to fuel speculation about a potential midseason move.

The Indian doubles veteran also reflected on his lifelong love for Wimbledon, calling it a dream tournament. With Novak Djokovic chasing a 25th Grand Slam and Iga Swiatek aiming for a successful title defence, Bopanna expects another highly competitive edition.

articles.title: ICC unveils post-pregnancy return-to-play guidelines for women cricketers
articles.description: The ICC has launched comprehensive guidelines to help female cricketers return to the sport post-pregnancy, recognizing the growing trend of mothers in elite cricket. This initiative, featuring a six-stage "6 Rs" model, aims to support players, coaches, and medical teams by offering practical advice on physical and mental recovery, alongside flexible arrangements. The move is lauded as a significant step for women's cricket, ensuring motherhood doesn't end a career.

articles.title: 'Speed up your calculation': D Gukesh advised ahead of World Chess Championship match against Javokhir Sindarov
articles.description: Ahead of his world title defence against Javokhir Sindarov, D Gukesh has been advised by Grandmaster Krishnan Sasikiran to improve his speed, technique and handling of time pressure. Sasikiran warned that Sindarov is highly prepared and fast-paced, but said Gukesh's previous world championship experience could prove crucial in what promises to be a tough contest.

articles.title: WC: Messi's Argentina, Mbappe's France in focus as final dash for knockouts begins
articles.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is entering a thrilling group stage finale with 42 nations vying for 29 spots in the Round of 32. Only USA, Mexico, and Germany have secured early qualification, while Haiti, Turkiye, and Tunisia are out. Powerhouses like Argentina, France, England, and Portugal are still fighting for advancement. The week promises intense competition and simultaneous matches as the knockout picture takes shape.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 | Is it time to worry for Belgium?
articles.description: Belgium face a must-win clash against New Zealand after drawing both of their opening FIFA World Cup 2026 matches. Despite boasting stars like Kevin De Bruyne, Romelu Lukaku and Jeremy Doku, the Red Devils are struggling to convert possession into goals.
